activities_projects.title: Visualizing the Poem
activities_projects.objective: Understand how the poet describes fog.
steps: Read the poem 'Fog'.
Draw how you imagine the fog moving over a city or harbour like a cat.
Label parts of your drawing with words from the poem.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and textual accuracy in drawing.

activities_projects.title: Identifying Literary Devices
activities_projects.objective: Recognize figurative language in the poem.
steps: Find examples of personification and metaphor (fog as a cat).
Write 2–3 sentences explaining why the poet uses these devices.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand poetic techniques and their effects.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification and explanation of devices.

activities_projects.title: Understanding the Mood
activities_projects.objective: Interpret the mood created by the poem.
steps: Discuss how the fog is described (silent, observing).
Write 3–4 sentences explaining the feeling or mood the poet evokes.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and textual support in explaining mood.

activities_projects.title: Comparing Fog to a Cat
activities_projects.objective: Explore the metaphor used in the poem.
steps: Discuss why the poet compares fog to a cat.
Write 5 sentences explaining how this makes the poem more vivid.

activities_projects.title: Creative Writing – Personification
steps: Choose a natural phenomenon (wind, rain, snow).
Write a short 5-line poem giving it human or animal-like qualities inspired by 'Fog'.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice creative use of figurative language.
activities_projects.evaluation: Originality, clarity, and proper use of personification.

activities_projects.title: Discussion on Minimalism
activities_projects.objective: Understand how few words create strong imagery.
steps: Read the poem aloud.
Discuss how the poet uses very few words but creates a clear picture.
Share examples of other short poems that paint a vivid image.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students appreciate minimalistic style in poetry.
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and examples shared during discussion.

activities_projects.title: Analytical Short Answer
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the structure and impact of the poem.
steps: Discuss why each line is short and precise.
Write 4–5 sentences explaining how line breaks and brevity contribute to the poem’s effect.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students interpret form and structure in poetry.

activities_projects.title: Role Play Activity
steps: One student acts as fog (moving silently).
Another student acts as the city/harbour.
Enact the poem slowly, emphasizing quiet movement and observation.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop expressive and interpretive skills.

activities_projects.title: Connecting to Nature
activities_projects.objective: Relate the poem to real-life experiences.
steps: Observe a foggy morning or evening.
Write 5 sentences describing the fog and how it made you feel, connecting to the poem.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect poetry to personal observation and experience.
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation accuracy and connection to poem.

activities_projects.title: Essay/Paragraph Writing
steps: Write a short essay (150–200 words) analyzing the poem’s imagery, personification, and mood.
Include examples and personal interpretation.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students synthesize literary elements in analytical writing.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis, use of textual evidence, clarity, and originality.
